• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Delegation as a Prosocial Act under Social Awareness: Revisiting the Intrinsic Value of Decision Rights

Tomo OKADA, Masao NAGATSUKA, and Toshihiko NANBA

  • This file reproduces the main results, figures, and appendix tables reported in the manuscript.

Load required packages

library(tidyverse)
library(zTree)
library(DT)
library(here)
library(fixest)
library(modelsummary)
library(readxl)

Load the original data from Bartling et al. (2014)

  • The results differ slightly from those reported in the paper, possibly because the session on 121206 contains missing values.
bfsdata <- read.csv(here("data", "Bartling_original", "Bartling_original.csv")) %>%
  filter(maecontrol == 0,
         type == "Principal")

datatable(
  bfsdata,
  extensions = "Scroller",
  options = list(
    deferRender = TRUE,
    scrollY = 200,
    scrollX = TRUE,
    scroller = TRUE
  )
)

Function for processing experimental data

bartling <- function(file_path) {
  
  data <- zTreeTables(file_path, ignore.errors = TRUE)
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Global data
  #-----------------------------
  data_global <- 
    data$globals %>% 
    filter(Treatment == 1) %>%
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "+\\[", replacement = "_") %>% 
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "]", replacement = "")
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Subject-level data
  #-----------------------------
  data_subjects <- data$subjects
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Questionnaire data
  #-----------------------------
  questionnaire <- 
    readxl::read_excel(here("data", "Quesstionnaire.xlsx")) %>%
    select(
      sessionid = session_id,
      subject,
      female,
      economics,
      q_gaze_next = q2_8,
      q_gaze_nearby = q2_9,
      q_gaze_experimenter = q2_10,
      q_analyzed_by_experimenter = q2_11
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      sessionid = as.character(sessionid),
      subject = as.numeric(subject)
    )
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Final profit
  #-----------------------------
  finalprofit <- 
    data_subjects %>% 
    filter(Treatment == max(Treatment)) %>%
    select(finalprofit = Profitm, subject = Subject)
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Loss gamble / IoC
  #-----------------------------
  loss_ioc <- 
    data_subjects %>%
    filter(Treatment == 4) %>%
    transmute(
      subject = Subject,
      lossgamble = lossgamble,
      IoC = IoC
    ) %>%
    distinct()
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Part 1
  #-----------------------------
  data1 <- 
    data_subjects %>%
    filter(Treatment == 1) %>%
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "+\\[", replacement = "_") %>% 
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "]", replacement = "") %>%
    mutate(
      showupfee = 1200,
      type = ifelse(Type == 1, "Principal", "Agent")
    ) %>%
    relocate(Treatment, subject = Subject, sessionid = Date, showupfee, type) %>%
    {
      if ("Group_1" %in% names(.)) select(., !Group_1:ncol(.)) else .
    } %>%
    select(!Period:slow) %>%
    inner_join(finalprofit, by = "subject")
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Reshape Part 1 into tidy format
  #-----------------------------
  data1 <- data1 %>%
    pivot_longer(
      !c(subject, sessionid, finalprofit, showupfee, type, Treatment),
      names_to = c(".value", "situation"),
      names_sep = "_"
    ) %>% 
    mutate(
      period_game = case_when(
        situation == 1 ~ data_global$O_1,
        situation == 2 ~ data_global$O_2,
        situation == 3 ~ data_global$O_3,
        situation == 4 ~ data_global$O_4,
        situation == 5 ~ data_global$O_5,
        situation == 6 ~ data_global$O_6,
        situation == 7 ~ data_global$O_7,
        situation == 8 ~ data_global$O_8,
        situation == 9 ~ data_global$O_9,
        situation == 10 ~ data_global$O_10
      ),
      va = case_when(
        situation == 1 ~ data_global$VA_1,
        situation == 2 ~ data_global$VA_2,
        situation == 3 ~ data_global$VA_3,
        situation == 4 ~ data_global$VA_4,
        situation == 5 ~ data_global$VA_5,
        situation == 6 ~ data_global$VA_6,
        situation == 7 ~ data_global$VA_7,
        situation == 8 ~ data_global$VA_8,
        situation == 9 ~ data_global$VA_9,
        situation == 10 ~ data_global$VA_10
      ),
      vb = case_when(
        situation == 1 ~ data_global$VB_1,
        situation == 2 ~ data_global$VB_2,
        situation == 3 ~ data_global$VB_3,
        situation == 4 ~ data_global$VB_4,
        situation == 5 ~ data_global$VB_5,
        situation == 6 ~ data_global$VB_6,
        situation == 7 ~ data_global$VB_7,
        situation == 8 ~ data_global$VB_8,
        situation == 9 ~ data_global$VB_9,
        situation == 10 ~ data_global$VB_10
      ),
      vc = case_when(
        situation == 1 ~ data_global$VC_1,
        situation == 2 ~ data_global$VC_2,
        situation == 3 ~ data_global$VC_3,
        situation == 4 ~ data_global$VC_4,
        situation == 5 ~ data_global$VC_5,
        situation == 6 ~ data_global$VC_6,
        situation == 7 ~ data_global$VC_7,
        situation == 8 ~ data_global$VC_8,
        situation == 9 ~ data_global$VC_9,
        situation == 10 ~ data_global$VC_10
      ),
      vd = case_when(
        situation == 1 ~ data_global$VD_1,
        situation == 2 ~ data_global$VD_2,
        situation == 3 ~ data_global$VD_3,
        situation == 4 ~ data_global$VD_4,
        situation == 5 ~ data_global$VD_5,
        situation == 6 ~ data_global$VD_6,
        situation == 7 ~ data_global$VD_7,
        situation == 8 ~ data_global$VD_8,
        situation == 9 ~ data_global$VD_9,
        situation == 10 ~ data_global$VD_10
      ),
      stakesize = ifelse(situation <= 5, "low",
                  ifelse(situation >= 6, "high", NA)),
      conflict = ifelse(situation == 1, "0.75(Low)",
                 ifelse(situation == 2, "0.75(Low)",
                 ifelse(situation == 3, "0.5(High)",
                 ifelse(situation == 4, "0.5(High)",
                 ifelse(situation == 5, "1(No)",
                 ifelse(situation == 6, "0.75(Low)",
                 ifelse(situation == 7, "0.75(Low)",
                 ifelse(situation == 8, "0.5(High)",
                 ifelse(situation == 9, "0.5(High)",
                 ifelse(situation == 10, "1(No)", NA)))))))))),
      cost = ifelse(type == "Principal", pcost, acost),
      situation = as.numeric(situation)
    ) %>%
    relocate(
      c(sessionid, subject, situation, 
        finalprofit, showupfee, type,
        period_game, aeffort, peffort,
        mae = MAE, varianta = VariantA, 
        variantp = VariantP)
    ) %>%
    select(!c(Treatment, acost, pcost))
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Part 2
  #-----------------------------
  data2 <- data_subjects %>%
    filter(Treatment == 2) %>%
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "+\\[", replacement = "_") %>%
    rename_all(gsub, pattern = "]", replacement = "") %>%
    select(Subject, OL_1:offeraccept_20) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      -Subject,
      names_to = c(".value", "situation"),
      names_sep = "_"
    ) %>%
    mutate(situation = as.numeric(situation))
  
  data2_control_lottery <- 
    data2 %>%
    filter(situation <= 10) %>%
    mutate(period_lottery = OL, subject = Subject) %>%
    select(!c(OL, Subject))
  
  data2_delegation_lottery <- 
    data2 %>%
    filter(situation > 10) %>%
    mutate(situation = situation - 10) %>%
    select(
      period_lottery_del = OL, 
      offeracceptd = offeraccept,
      subject = Subject, 
      situation
    )
  
  data2 <- 
    inner_join(
      data2_control_lottery, 
      data2_delegation_lottery, 
      by = c("subject", "situation")
    ) %>%
    relocate(period_lottery, period_lottery_del, offeraccept, offeracceptd) %>%
    filter(offeraccept != 0 & offeraccept != 1)
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Merge Part 1 and Part 2
  #-----------------------------
  x <- 
    inner_join(data1, data2, by = c("subject", "situation")) %>%
    left_join(loss_ioc, by = "subject") %>%
    mutate(
      IV = ifelse(type == "Principal", offeracceptd - offeraccept, NA)
    ) %>%
    select(-j)
  
  #-----------------------------
  # Merge questionnaire data
  #-----------------------------
  x <- 
    x %>%
    mutate(
      sessionid = as.character(sessionid),
      subject = as.numeric(subject)
    ) %>%
    left_join(questionnaire, by = c("sessionid", "subject"))
  
  return(x)
}

Load and prepare the experimental data

Treatment Description.

  • T1: Partitioned and unidentified counterpart
  • T2: No partition and unidentified counterpart
  • T3: Partitioned and identified counterpart
  • T4: No partition and identified counterpart

Participants: 324 subjects (3236 observations).

  • T1: 42 pairs (419 observations; 1 observation dropped because offeraccept was 0 or 1)
  • T2: 39 pairs (387 observations; 3 observations dropped because offeraccept was 0 or 1)
  • T3: 42 pairs (420 observations)
  • T4: 39 pairs (390 observations)
data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    total_obs = n()
  )
  • Display the dataset
tidy_data %>%
  datatable(
    data =., 
    #filter = 'bottom',
    extensions = 'Scroller',
    options = list(
    deferRender = TRUE,
    scrollY = 200,
    scrollX = TRUE,
    scroller = TRUE
  ))

Analysis

4.2 Intrinsic Value of Decision Rights (IV)

# Function to create bar plots by treatment

treatment_bar <- 
  function (df, var, num1, num2) {
      x <- df %>%
            group_by(treatment) %>%
            ggplot(aes(x= treatment, 
                       y = {{ var }})) +
            coord_cartesian(ylim=c(num1, num2)) +
            stat_summary(geom = "col", fun = "mean", fill = "grey50", width = 0.5) +  
            stat_summary( fun.data = "mean_se", 
                          geom = "errorbar", 
                          width = .2, color = "grey20") +
            theme_bw() +
            labs(x = "Treatment") +
            theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
      
      return(x)
  }
  • Descriptive statistics of IV by treatment, including Bartling et al. (2014)
# Combine IV data from our experiment with Bartling et al. (2014)

data_main <- data %>%
  select(IV, mae, peffort, treatment)

bfsdata_main <- bfsdata %>%
  select(IV, mae, peffort) %>%
  mutate(treatment = "Bartling et al. (2014)")

data_main_joined <- rbind(data_main, bfsdata_main) 

# Summary statistics by treatment
data_main_joined  %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_IV = mean(IV, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_IV   = sd(IV, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = sum(!is.na(IV)),
    se_IV   = sd_IV / sqrt(n)
  )
  • Measured intrinsic value of decision rights (IV) by treatment (Figure 2)
# Bar plot of IV by treatment (with standard error bars)

data %>%
  treatment_bar(., IV, 0, 45)

Measured IV: T1 vs T4

  • We first compare the measured intrinsic value of decision rights (IV) between T1 and T4 using a two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test.
  • The null hypothesis of equal distributions is rejected (\(W = 95{,}606\), \(p < 0.001\)).
  • IV is significantly higher in T1 than in T4, consistent with H2P rather than H2R.
# Extract observations for T1 and T4 only
df_T1T4 <- data %>%
  filter(treatment %in% c(1, 4)) %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment))

# Two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test
wilcox.test(
  IV ~ treatment,
  data = df_T1T4,
  exact = FALSE
)
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  IV by treatment
## W = 95606, p-value = 2.761e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0

Regression analysis for measured IV

  • We next estimate regression models for measured IV.
  • Columns (1) and (2) use treatment indicators with T1 as the reference category.
  • Columns (3) and (4) decompose the treatment effects into the two treatment dimensions: openness (open) and counterpart identification (identified).
  • Following Ferreira et al. (2020), Columns (2) and (4) additionally control for gender, a dummy for students in Economics or Management, and behavioral measures from the Loss Aversion and Illusion of Control tasks.
# Define treatment dimensions: openness (no partition) and counterpart identification
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    open = ifelse(treatment %in% c(2, 4), 1, 0),
    identified = ifelse(treatment %in% c(3, 4), 1, 0)
  )

# Treatment-based specifications
res_tr1 <- feols(
  IV ~ i(treatment, ref = 1),
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)

res_tr2 <- feols(
  IV ~ i(treatment, ref = 1) + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)

# Mechanism-based specifications
res_mech1 <- feols(
  IV ~ open * identified,
  data = data,
  cluster = ~participant
)

res_mech2 <- feols(
  IV ~ open * identified + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
  data = data,
  cluster = ~participant
)

# Regression table (displayed and exported)
modelsummary(
  list(
    "(1) Treatment" = res_tr1,
    "(2) Treatment" = res_tr2,
    "(3) Mechanism" = res_mech1,
    "(4) Mechanism" = res_mech2
  ),
  coef_rename = c(
    "treatment::2" = "T2",
    "treatment::3" = "T3",
    "treatment::4" = "T4",
    "lossgamble" = "Loss Aversion",
    "IoC" = "Illusion of Control",
    "female" = "Female",
    "economics" = "Economics & Management"
  ),
  coef_order = c(
    "treatment::2",
    "treatment::3",
    "treatment::4",
    "open",
    "identified",
    "open:identified",
    "lossgamble",
    "IoC",
    "female",
    "economics"
  ),
  stars = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01),
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj"
)
(1) Treatment (2) Treatment (3) Mechanism (4) Mechanism
* p < 0.1, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01
(Intercept) 39.659*** 36.058*** 39.659*** 36.058***
(6.217) (9.351) (6.217) (9.351)
T2 -13.788* -15.017*
(7.859) (8.100)
T3 -16.430** -13.107*
(7.622) (7.733)
T4 -20.225*** -18.779**
(7.275) (7.383)
Loss Aversion 0.467 0.467
(1.307) (1.307)
Illusion of Control 0.013 0.013
(0.333) (0.333)
Female -3.108 -3.108
(5.153) (5.153)
Economics & Management 16.542** 16.542**
(6.885) (6.885)
open -13.788* -15.017*
(7.859) (8.100)
identified -16.430** -13.107*
(7.622) (7.733)
open:identified 9.993 9.345
(9.771) (9.834)
Num.Obs. 1616 1576 1616 1576
R2 0.013 0.021 0.013 0.021
RMSE 66.99 66.80 66.99 66.80
Std.Errors by: participant by: participant by: participant by: participant

H3 test: positivity of IV in all treatments

  • Finally, we test Hypothesis 3 by examining whether the mean measured IV is significantly greater than zero in each treatment.
  • We report the treatment-level mean IV, the raw p-value from a one-sample t-test against zero, and the Bonferroni-adjusted p-value.
  • The mean IV is significantly positive in all treatments (all Bonferroni-adjusted \(p < 0.001\)), supporting H3.
# H3 test: positivity of IV in all treatments
data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_IV = mean(IV, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_raw = t.test(IV, mu = 0)$p.value
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_raw, method = "bonferroni")
  )

4.3 Delegation Threshold (e)

  • Descriptive statistics of minimum effort requirement (e) by treatment, including Bartling et al. (2014)
# Summary statistics by treatment
data_main_joined  %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_mae = mean(mae, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_mae   = sd(mae, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = sum(!is.na(mae)),
    se_mae   = sd_mae / sqrt(n)
  )
  • Minimum effort requirement (e) by treatment (Figure 3)
# Bar plot of MAE by treatment (with standard error bars)
data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  treatment_bar(., mae, 45, 65) +
  theme_bw()

Minimum effor requirement e: T1 vs T4

  • We next compare the minimum effort requirement (e) between T1 and T4 using a two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test.
  • The null hypothesis of equal distributions is rejected (\(W = 97{,}040\), \(p < 0.001\)).
  • e is significantly higher in T1 than in T4, consistent with H2P rather than H2R.
# Two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test
wilcox.test(
  mae ~ treatment,
  data = df_T1T4,
  exact = FALSE
)
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  mae by treatment
## W = 97040, p-value = 3.743e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0

Regression analysis for minimum effort requirement (e)

  • We estimate regression models for the minimum effort requirement (e).
  • The set of explanatory variables is identical to that used in the IV regressions.
# Treatment-based specifications
res_tr1 <- feols(
  mae ~ i(treatment, ref = 1),
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)

res_tr2 <- feols(
  mae ~ i(treatment, ref = 1) + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)

# Mechanism-based specifications
res_mech1 <- feols(
  mae ~ open * identified,
  data = data,
  cluster = ~participant
)

res_mech2 <- feols(
  mae ~ open * identified + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
  data = data,
  cluster = ~participant
)

# Regression table
modelsummary(
  list(
    "(1) Treatment" = res_tr1,
    "(2) Treatment" = res_tr2,
    "(3) Mechanism" = res_mech1,
    "(4) Mechanism" = res_mech2
  ),
  coef_rename = c(
    "treatment::2" = "T2",
    "treatment::3" = "T3",
    "treatment::4" = "T4",
    "lossgamble" = "Loss Aversion",
    "IoC" = "Illusion of Control",
    "female" = "Female",
    "economics" = "Economics & Management"
  ),
  coef_order = c(
    "treatment::2",
    "treatment::3",
    "treatment::4",
    "open",
    "identified",
    "open:identified",
    "lossgamble",
    "IoC",
    "female",
    "economics"
  ),
  stars = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01),
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj"
)
(1) Treatment (2) Treatment (3) Mechanism (4) Mechanism
* p < 0.1, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01
(Intercept) 61.492*** 59.834*** 61.492*** 59.834***
(2.690) (4.063) (2.690) (4.063)
T2 -5.060 -5.927
(3.993) (4.067)
T3 -7.089* -6.527
(4.135) (4.288)
T4 -7.348** -7.793**
(3.561) (3.633)
Loss Aversion 0.656 0.656
(0.798) (0.798)
Illusion of Control 0.106 0.106
(0.158) (0.158)
Female -2.819 -2.819
(2.956) (2.956)
Economics & Management 2.453 2.453
(3.425) (3.425)
open -5.060 -5.927
(3.993) (4.067)
identified -7.089* -6.527
(4.135) (4.288)
open:identified 4.801 4.661
(5.590) (5.664)
Num.Obs. 1616 1576 1616 1576
R2 0.012 0.020 0.012 0.020
RMSE 27.19 27.15 27.19 27.15
Std.Errors by: participant by: participant by: participant by: participant

4.4 Ruling Out Alternative Explanations

  • Descriptive statistics of principal/s effort level (E) by treatment, including Bartling et al. (2014)
# Summary statistics by treatment
data_main_joined  %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_peffort = mean(peffort, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_peffort   = sd(peffort, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = sum(!is.na(peffort)),
    se_peffort   = sd_peffort / sqrt(n)
  )
  • Principal’s effort level (E) by treatment (Figure 4)
# Bar plot of MAE by treatment (with standard error bars)

data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  treatment_bar(., peffort, 50, 70) +
  theme_bw()

Regression analysis for principal’s effort level (E)

  • We estimate regression models for principal’s effort level (E)).
  • The set of explanatory variables is identical to that used in the IV regressions.
# Treatment-based specifications
res_tr1 <- feols(
  peffort ~ i(treatment, ref = 1),
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)

res_tr2 <- feols(
  peffort ~ i(treatment, ref = 1) + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
  data = data,
  cluster = "participant"
)


# Mechanism-based specifications
res_mech1<- feols(peffort ~ open * identified,
      data = data,
      cluster = ~participant)

res_mech2<- feols(peffort ~ open * identified + lossgamble + IoC + female + economics,
      data = data,
      cluster = ~participant)

# Regression table
modelsummary(
  list(
    "(1) Treatment" = res_tr1,
    "(2) Treatment" = res_tr2,
    "(3) Mechanism" = res_mech1,
    "(4) Mechanism" = res_mech2
  ),
  coef_rename = c(
    "treatment::2" = "T2",
    "treatment::3" = "T3",
    "treatment::4" = "T4",
    "lossgamble" = "Loss Aversion",
    "IoC" = "Illusion of Control",
    "female" = "Female",
    "economics" = "Economics & Management"
  ),
  coef_order = c(
    "treatment::2",
    "treatment::3",
    "treatment::4",
    "open",
    "identified",
    "open:identified",
    "lossgamble",
    "IoC"
  ),
  stars = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01),
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj"
)
(1) Treatment (2) Treatment (3) Mechanism (4) Mechanism
* p < 0.1, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01
(Intercept) 63.329*** 63.230*** 63.329*** 63.230***
(2.291) (3.296) (2.291) (3.296)
T2 -2.572 -2.915
(3.329) (3.337)
T3 -1.303 -1.769
(3.080) (3.144)
T4 -0.445 -0.443
(3.112) (3.245)
Loss Aversion 0.223 0.223
(0.574) (0.574)
Illusion of Control 0.206* 0.206*
(0.119) (0.119)
Female -1.561 -1.561
(2.406) (2.406)
Economics & Management -2.424 -2.424
(3.507) (3.507)
open -2.572 -2.915
(3.329) (3.337)
identified -1.303 -1.769
(3.080) (3.144)
open:identified 3.431 4.240
(4.444) (4.473)
Num.Obs. 1616 1576 1616 1576
R2 0.002 0.009 0.002 0.009
RMSE 22.33 22.36 22.33 22.36
Std.Errors by: participant by: participant by: participant by: participant
  • The Wald test examines whether all treatment effects (T2–T4 relative to T1) are jointly equal to zero.
  • The null cannot be rejected (\(p = 0.872\)), indicating no overall treatment effect on effort relative to T1.
wald(res_tr1, keep = "treatment")
## Wald test, H0: joint nullity of treatment::2, treatment::3 and treatment::4
##  stat = 0.234925, p-value = 0.872065, on 3 and 1,612 DoF, VCOV: Clustered (participant).

Appendix A: Self-reported awareness of others

Post-experimental questionnaire items (social awareness measures).

  • Q1: During the experiment, were you concerned about the presence or gaze of the participant sitting next to you?
  • Q2: During the experiment, were you concerned about the presence or gaze of other nearby participants, excluding the one sitting next to you?
  • Q3: During the experiment, were you concerned about the presence or gaze of the experiment staff?
  • Q4: During the experiment, were you concerned that the experimenters would later observe data on your behavior?

  • Responses were recorded on a seven-point Likert scale, where 1 indicates “not at all concerned,” 4 indicates a neutral response, and 7 indicates “very concerned.”

  • All statistics are based on responses from participants in the role of principals.

  • The table reports treatment-level means of each item (with standard deviations in parentheses). The rightmost column reports p-values from ANOVA tests of equality across treatments.

#--------------------------
# 1. Descriptive statistics
#--------------------------
# Compute means, standard deviations, and sample sizes by treatment

desc_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    q_gaze_next_mean  = mean(q_gaze_next, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_next_sd    = sd(q_gaze_next, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_next_n     = sum(!is.na(q_gaze_next)),

    q_gaze_nearby_mean  = mean(q_gaze_nearby, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_nearby_sd    = sd(q_gaze_nearby, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_nearby_n     = sum(!is.na(q_gaze_nearby)),

    q_gaze_experimenter_mean = mean(q_gaze_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_experimenter_sd   = sd(q_gaze_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_experimenter_n    = sum(!is.na(q_gaze_experimenter)),

    q_analyzed_by_experimenter_mean = mean(q_analyzed_by_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_analyzed_by_experimenter_sd   = sd(q_analyzed_by_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_analyzed_by_experimenter_n    = sum(!is.na(q_analyzed_by_experimenter)),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

#--------------------------
# 2. ANOVA
#--------------------------
# Test whether treatment differences are statistically significant

anova_q_gaze_next <- summary(aov(q_gaze_next ~ factor(treatment), data = data))
anova_q_gaze_nearby <- summary(aov(q_gaze_nearby ~ factor(treatment), data = data))
anova_q_gaze_experimenter <- summary(aov(q_gaze_experimenter ~ factor(treatment), data = data))
anova_q_analyzed_by_experimenter <- summary(aov(q_analyzed_by_experimenter ~ factor(treatment), data = data))

# Extract p-values
p_q_gaze_next <- anova_q_gaze_next[[1]]["factor(treatment)", "Pr(>F)"]
p_q_gaze_nearby <- anova_q_gaze_nearby[[1]]["factor(treatment)", "Pr(>F)"]
p_q_gaze_experimenter <- anova_q_gaze_experimenter[[1]]["factor(treatment)", "Pr(>F)"]
p_q_analyzed_by_experimenter <- anova_q_analyzed_by_experimenter[[1]]["factor(treatment)", "Pr(>F)"]

#--------------------------
# 3. Appendix table: means
#--------------------------
# Prepare treatment-level means for each variable

appendix_means <- data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    q_gaze_next = mean(q_gaze_next, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_nearby = mean(q_gaze_nearby, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_experimenter = mean(q_gaze_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_analyzed_by_experimenter = mean(q_analyzed_by_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(q_gaze_next, q_gaze_nearby, q_gaze_experimenter, q_analyzed_by_experimenter),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "mean"
  ) %>%
  mutate(treatment = paste0("T", treatment)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = treatment,
    values_from = mean
  )

#--------------------------
# 4. Appendix table: standard deviations
#--------------------------
# Prepare treatment-level standard deviations

appendix_sds <- data %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    q_gaze_next = sd(q_gaze_next, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_nearby = sd(q_gaze_nearby, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_gaze_experimenter = sd(q_gaze_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_analyzed_by_experimenter = sd(q_analyzed_by_experimenter, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(q_gaze_next, q_gaze_nearby, q_gaze_experimenter, q_analyzed_by_experimenter),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "sd"
  ) %>%
  mutate(treatment = paste0("T", treatment)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = treatment,
    values_from = sd,
    names_prefix = "sd_"
  )

#--------------------------
# 5. Appendix table: p-values
#--------------------------
# Store ANOVA p-values

appendix_p <- data.frame(
  variable = c("q_gaze_next", "q_gaze_nearby", "q_gaze_experimenter", "q_analyzed_by_experimenter"),
  p_value = c(p_q_gaze_next, p_q_gaze_nearby, p_q_gaze_experimenter, p_q_analyzed_by_experimenter)
)

#--------------------------
# 6. Final appendix table
#--------------------------
# Combine means, standard deviations, and p-values

appendix_table <- appendix_means %>%
  left_join(appendix_sds, by = "variable") %>%
  left_join(appendix_p, by = "variable") %>%
  mutate(
    variable = recode(variable,
      "q_gaze_next" = "Q1",
      "q_gaze_nearby" = "Q2",
      "q_gaze_experimenter" = "Q3",
      "q_analyzed_by_experimenter" = "Q4"
    ),
    T1 = paste0(round(T1, 2), " (", round(sd_T1, 2), ")"),
    T2 = paste0(round(T2, 2), " (", round(sd_T2, 2), ")"),
    T3 = paste0(round(T3, 2), " (", round(sd_T3, 2), ")"),
    T4 = paste0(round(T4, 2), " (", round(sd_T4, 2), ")"),
    p_value = round(p_value, 3)
  ) %>%
  select(variable, T1, T2, T3, T4, p_value)

appendix_table
sessionInfo()
